title: Date comparison returns incorrect results
poc: CREATE TABLE datepoint (mydate DATE, insiderange BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, rangename CHAR(8));
CREATE TABLE daterange (startdate DATE, enddate DATE, name CHAR(8));
INSERT INTO daterange (startdate, enddate, name) VALUES ('2012-03-01','2012-03-31','A');
select * from datepoint A, daterange B where A.mydate between B.startdate and B.enddate;
key_bug_features: Date comparison in SQL query between two tables
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE datepoint (mydate DATE, insiderange BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, rangename CHAR(8), id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY);
INSERT INTO datepoint (mydate, insiderange, rangename) VALUES ('2012-05-09', TRUE, 'Range1');
INSERT INTO datepoint (mydate, insiderange, rangename) VALUES ('2012-03-09', FALSE, 'Range2');
CREATE TABLE daterange (startdate DATE NOT NULL, enddate DATE NOT NULL, name CHAR(8), CONSTRAINT valid_range CHECK (startdate < enddate));
INSERT INTO daterange (startdate, enddate, name) VALUES ('2012-03-01', '2012-03-31', 'A');
SELECT A.mydate, B.name FROM datepoint A JOIN daterange B ON A.mydate BETWEEN B.startdate AND B.enddate;
